question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ErrNotImplemented` response from `MempoolTransaction()` to cause a Rosetta-based block explorer to display incorrect 'first seen' timestamps for transactions, since the explorer cannot verify when a transaction entered the mempool, allowing the attacker to make transactions appear to have been submitted at a different time than they actually were?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any IP-based or session-based rate limiting in `mempoolAPIService` to perform a slowloris-style attack on the `/mempool` endpoint, holding connections open and preventing legitimate block-history queries from being processed, creating apparent gaps in transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `MempoolTransaction()` never emits any metrics or telemetry (no instrumentation in the handler) to probe the endpoint repeatedly without triggering any anomaly detection, allowing the attacker to map out transaction confirmation timing and use that information to submit transactions in a specific order that reorganizes history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ErrNotImplemented` response from `Mempool()` to cause a Rosetta client's transaction ordering algorithm — which uses mempool data to determine submission sequence — to fall back to a default ordering that the attacker can predict and exploit to insert transactions at specific positions in block history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the missing `TransactionIdentifier` format validation in `MempoolTransaction()` to send a request with a hash containing special characters (e.g., null bytes, Unicode), causing the Rosetta SDK's response serializer to produce malformed JSON that corrupts the transaction history log of any client parsing the response?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `Mempool()` always returns the same static `ErrNotImplemented` error object (a package-level variable `errors.ErrNotImplemented`) to cause a race condition if any code path mutates the error's `Details` map concurrently, corrupting error responses across all concurrent mempool requests?
